content: harvest.sh --status crashes on empty .project-registry-meta.jsonl. Root cause: grep -F on empty file exits 1 under set -o pipefail. Fix: Added [ -s file ] check and || true to grep pipeline
Test suite aborts after first failure block. Root cause: set -e in run-tests.sh kills script when test function returns 1. Fix: Pre-existing issue — test invocations need || true wrapping
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Services must be listed with appropriate metadata tags." — The term "appropriate" is subjective and not objectively verifiable.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "API must return a status within one check interval of adding or updating a service." — The term "one check interval" is unclear and could vary, making it not objectively measurable.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The criterion "Service uptime status is updated in real-time based on check results" is not objectively verifiable. The term "real-time" is vague and requires a specific threshold to determine how quickly the status must be updated.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The criterion "HTTP methods, headers, and body checks are correctly implemented" in the `done_when` section uses the term "correctly", which is subjective and not objectively verifiable without specific conditions or examples.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The done_when item "Integration for each channel has been successfully tested" lacks specificity on what constitutes a successful test, making it subjective.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The done_when item "Alerts are logged and verified as sent for every service failure" needs clearer specifications about what verification entails and how it can be measured without human judgment.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The criterion "Real-time updates are reflected immediately upon check execution" is not objectively verifiable as it uses the term "immediately," which is subjective and does not specify an exact time frame for updates to appear.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The done_when item "Dashboard visually distinguishes between up, down, and degraded services" is subjective due to "visually distinguishes" without specific measurable criteria or examples.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The fail_when item "Delays in data reflection on the dashboard" is subjective as it does not define what qualifies as a delay.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The fail_when item "Inaccuracies in status or incident displays" requires human judgment to determine what qualifies as an "inaccuracy" without specifying objective tests or thresholds.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Users can view past incidents and uptime trends." - Not objectively verifiable. Needs specific conditions or examples of what "viewing" entails.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Interface allows for selection of specific date ranges for detailed analysis." - Not objectively verifiable. Needs definition of "detailed analysis" and specific UI elements.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Visualization of data with uptime percentages and incident counts." - Not specific enough; needs measurable criteria for visualization.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Historical incident data is correctly recorded and retrievable." - Requires specific definition of "correctly."
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Graphical representation of trends reflect recorded data accurately." - Uses subjective term "accurately" without objective measure.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "UI supports user interactions for arbitrary date range selection and visualization." - Not objectively verifiable due to subjective description of "supports."

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Clear user interface for management and overview of maintenance periods." is subjective. The term "clear" is not objectively verifiable as it requires human judgment to determine clarity.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Scheduled maintenance is visually highlighted on dashboard." lacks specificity on what constitutes "visually highlighted" and can be interpreted differently by different developers.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "UI allows for creating, viewing, editing, and deleting maintenance windows." is subjective as it does not provide specific conditions under which these actions are successful.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Incidents during scheduled periods carry a maintenance tag and do not trigger alerts." needs more detail on how the maintenance tag is applied and verified.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Services must be listed with appropriate metadata tags." - The term "appropriate" is subjective and requires definition of what constitutes appropriate metadata tags, making this criterion not objectively verifiable.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "API must return a status within one check interval of adding or updating a service." - Lacks specificity about what constitutes a "check interval," making it unclear without more context or definition.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: Acceptance criteria "Support for HTTP method, headers, expected status codes, and optional response body validation in checks" is too vague. It doesn't specify what constitutes "support" or how it's verified.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The done_when criteria "Up and down status transitions are recorded and stored." lacks specificity on how these transitions should be recorded and what storage mechanism should be used.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The done_when criteria "HTTP methods, headers, and body checks are correctly implemented." uses subjective language "correctly" which is non-verifiable.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: Technical Approach suggests "robust error handling," which is subjective and not part of the acceptance criteria but might influence implementation expectations.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The criterion "Integration for each channel has been successfully tested" is not objectively verifiable without human judgment; it lacks specific, measurable conditions for success.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The criterion "Alerts are not received when a service is down" requires subjective monitoring and judgment, as it does not specify how the alert receipt should be verified.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The criterion "Alerts are sent more than the configured number of retries" should specify the exact number of retries to make it objectively verifiable.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The criterion "Timeline events are logged for each incident state change." lacks specificity on what constitutes sufficient logging. It's not clear how this can be verified without subjective judgment.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The criterion "Incident duration metrics are calculated from open to resolved." is not specific enough about what metrics should be calculated and how they will be measured or verified.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The criteria in the `done_when` section are not sufficiently detailed to be objectively verifiable. For example, "Comprehensive audit log available for each incident" uses subjective language ("comprehensive") that is open to interpretation.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The fail_when criteria use specific examples, but some necessary criteria like "Timeline events do not reflect actual state changes or operator actions." could still use more concrete definitions or examples.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: Dashboard visually distinguishes between up, down, and degraded services: The term "visually distinguishes" is subjective and not objectively verifiable without defining specific visual elements (e.g., colors, icons).

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: Dashboard allows for environment/tag-based filtering with responsive UI: The term "responsive UI" is subjective. Responsiveness should be defined in measurable terms (e.g., response time in milliseconds).

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: Integration with backend services enables real-time dashboard updates: Simply stating integration with backend services does not provide specific conditions to verify real-time updates objectively.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Users can view past incidents and uptime trends" lacks specific conditions that define what it means to "view" them. It is subjective and not sufficiently concrete for automated testing.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Interface allows for selection of specific date ranges for detailed analysis" does not specify how this selection should function, making it hard to objectively verify.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Visualization of data with uptime percentages and incident counts" lacks detail on what constitutes correct visualization, leaving room for subjective judgment.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Historical incident data is correctly recorded and retrievable" does not specify how correctness is determined or verified.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Graphical representation of trends reflect recorded data accurately" relies on subjective interpretation of accuracy.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "UI supports user interactions for arbitrary date range selection and visualization" is vague and subjective without specific conditions.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The criterion "Clear user interface for management and overview of maintenance periods" uses subjective language. What is considered "clear" is not objectively verifiable without human judgment.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The done_when item "Scheduled maintenance is visually highlighted on dashboard" is subjective since what qualifies as "visually highlighted" is open to interpretation and requires human judgment.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: Criteria "Services must be listed with appropriate metadata tags" uses subjective language ("appropriate") and lacks specific, objective verification methods.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: Criteria "API must return a status within one check interval of adding or updating a service" is vague. "One check interval" is not defined, making it difficult to verify objectively.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Scheduled checks are run based on user-configured intervals." lacks specificity on how intervals should be validated - e.g., time frames or methods.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Service uptime status is updated in real-time based on check results." is subjective; "real-time" isn't defined and can vary greatly in interpretation.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Support for HTTP method, headers, expected status codes, and optional response body validation in checks." lacks concrete testing criteria and expected results for validation.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "All configured services automatically have configured checks run." implies automation but lacks specifics on how this automation is verified.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Up and down status transitions are recorded and stored." needs clear criteria on what qualifies as "up" or "down" and how transition recording is verified.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "HTTP methods, headers, and body checks are correctly implemented." is vague; what "correctly" means needs definition in terms of expected outcomes for objective evaluation.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Alerts are issued when a service fails a check" - This is not specific enough and does not mention any specific condition or how this will be verified.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Alerts can be configured to send via email, webhooks, Telegram, Slack, and Discord" - Needs specific details on what configuration options must be available and how they will be verified.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Channel integration settings are provided in user-settings." - Lacks detail on what specific settings should be provided and how they can be verified.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Alerts are logged and verified as sent for every service failure" - Does not specify the logging details or how the verification process is handled.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Supports retry with exponential backoff for failed alert sends" - Needs details on what constitutes a "failed alert send" and specific timing for the backoff.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Integration for each channel has been successfully tested" - Unclear how success is defined or measured in the context of testing integrations.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The phrase "Timeline events are logged for each incident state change" is not specific enough about what constitutes a sufficient level of logging, and does not specify how it should be verified.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Incident duration metrics are calculated from open to resolved" lacks specificity on what metrics are calculated and how they should be measured or verified.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Comprehensive audit log available for each incident" is subjective due to "comprehensive", which is open to interpretation and lacks measurable criteria.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Dashboard visually distinguishes between up, down, and degraded services" - This criterion uses subjective language as "visually distinguishes" requires human judgment to verify and is not objectively verifiable.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Dashboard allows for environment/tag-based filtering with responsive UI" - The term "responsive UI" is subjective and requires a measurable definition to be objectively verifiable.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Integration with backend services enables real-time dashboard updates" - This is not specific enough to be objectively verifiable without further measurable conditions.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Users can view past incidents and uptime trends." - This criterion is too vague. It does not specify what "view" means concretely or how it can be tested. Detailed conditions for verification are missing.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Interface allows for selection of specific date ranges for detailed analysis." - The criterion lacks specific details about the interface requirements and verification method.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Visualization of data with uptime percentages and incident counts." - This criterion does not define what constitutes correct visualization or how it should be verified.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Historical incident data is correctly recorded and retrievable." - "Correctly recorded" is subjective without defining what "correctly" means in measurable terms.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Graphical representation of trends reflect recorded data accurately." - "Accurately" is subjective without specific metrics or methods of verification.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "UI supports user interactions for arbitrary date range selection and visualization." - The term "supports" needs to be quantified or defined in a testable manner to remove subjectivity.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The criterion "Clear user interface for management and overview of maintenance periods" uses subjective language ("Clear").

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The done_when item "Scheduled maintenance is visually highlighted on dashboard" can be subjective if "visually highlighted" is not defined with exact visual conditions and standards.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The done_when item "UI allows for creating, viewing, editing, and deleting maintenance windows" lacks specific conditions on how the UI presents these actions and their expected outcomes.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Comprehensive audit log available for each incident." is not objectively verifiable. The term "comprehensive" is subjective and could be interpreted differently by different developers.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The fail_when condition "Delays in data reflection on the dashboard" needs a quantified measure of acceptable delay.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Inaccuracies in status or incident displays" is subjective because it lacks specific metrics or conditions to determine accuracy.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The criterion "Integration for each channel has been successfully tested" lacks specificity on what qualifies as a successful test, making it subjective.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: Criterion "Incident duration metrics are calculated from open to resolved" is vague. It does not specify how the metrics should be calculated, what precision is required, or any specific conditions that must be met.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: Criterion "Comprehensive audit log available for each incident" is not objectively verifiable. It lacks specifics on what "comprehensive" means — what details must be included in the audit log.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Scheduled checks are run based on user-configured intervals." lacks specific conditions on how intervals are defined and measured.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "HTTP methods, headers, and body checks are correctly implemented." contains subjective language with "correctly implemented" without concrete conditions.
MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The criterion "Alerts are issued when a service fails a check" is not objectively verifiable as it doesn't specify how to determine or measure the alert issuance.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The acceptance criterion "Incident duration metrics are calculated from open to resolved" lacks specificity and a measurable threshold to ensure objectivity. It does not clarify how the duration is measured or the accuracy required.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: "Integration with backend services enables real-time dashboard updates." is vague without specifying what constitutes successful integration.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The criterion "Users can view past incidents and uptime trends" is not specific enough. It doesn't define what "view" entails or what qualifies as sufficient viewing functionality.

MG0 ac-validator FAIL: The criterion "Visualization of data with uptime percentages and incident counts" is not verifiable as it lacks details on accuracy requirements or layout specifications.
